title: Market Brief — Thursday, April 23, 2026
summary: Compute is in severe shortage across all three GPU classes: H100 at 11% available (110/343), B200 at 13% (129/419), and H200 at 19% (57/315). The standout price move is H200 spiking 17.7% day-over-day to $4.52/hr median spot, with annualized volatility at 57.5%, signaling erratic demand surges as workloads migrate off legacy A100s whose utilization cratered 10.6pp in 30 days.
paragraphs: Compute is in severe shortage across all three GPU classes: H100 at 11% available (110/343), B200 at 13% (129/419), and H200 at 19% (57/315). The standout price move is H200 spiking 17.7% day-over-day to $4.52/hr median spot, with annualized volatility at 57.5%, signaling erratic demand surges as workloads migrate off legacy A100s whose utilization cratered 10.6pp in 30 days.

B200 is the only profitable inference GPU at medium-tier pricing, scraping a 3% margin against a TCO-adjusted cost of $3.65/M tokens; H200 operators selling at medium tier are underwater, losing roughly $11.81/M tokens against a $16.31 TCO-adjusted cost. Provider grades: DeepSeek Strong (V3.2 at $0.378 output, brutal efficiency), OpenAI Neutral (wide portfolio hedges margin risk), Anthropic Neutral (Opus premium holds but Haiku compresses), Google Weak (Gemini 2.5 Pro priced at $10 output with no cost moat), Meta Burning Cash (Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60 output is a loss-leader land grab).

H200 spot at $4.52/hr is overvalued given 76.1% B200 volatility offers better performance per dollar at $4.00/hr with a larger available pool. Undervalued trade: accumulate B200 spot capacity now at $3.63 floor while severe shortage persists, and sell inference at premium tier for 94% margins before supply normalizes.
bullets: 11% of H100 GPUs are available, the tightest supply across all classes.
57 of 315 H200 GPUs are free, keeping availability at a scarce 19%.
$4.52/hr H200 median spot is up 17.7% in a single day.
21.41 LLMflation index means API prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.378/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 is 158x cheaper than OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M.
86.6% B200 utilization surged 49.2 percentage points in 30 days, the fastest ramp in the dataset.
